statute:us:section:1983.title: 42 U.S.C. § 1983 – Civil action for deprivation of rights
statute:us:section:1983.description: Provides a civil remedy against persons acting under color of state law who violate federal rights.

cases.title: Marbury v. Madison
cases.court: Supreme Court of the United States
cases.summary: Foundational case for judicial review that narrates federal remedies; later citations used Marbury when statutes engaged federal rights.

cases.title: Monroe v. Pape
cases.court: Supreme Court of the United States
cases.summary: Held § 1983 provides a remedy even when statute does not explicitly create a private right; it anchors modern civil-rights litigation.

statute:us:section:501.title: 18 U.S.C. § 501 – Official misconduct
statute:us:section:501.description: Defines misconduct committed by federal or state officials.

cases.title: United States v. Siegelman
cases.court: U.S. Court of Appeals, Eleventh Circuit
cases.summary: Examined § 501 while reviewing allegations that public officials misused office resources; demonstrates statutory enforcement in campaign contexts.

statute:us:section:1988.title: 42 U.S.C. § 1988 – Proceedings in vindication of civil rights
statute:us:section:1988.description: Authorizes courts to award attorneys' fees in § 1983 and other civil rights cases.

cases.title: Backlund v. Bowman
cases.court: Supreme Court of the United States
cases.summary: Held that statutes of limitations are governed by state law for § 1983 actions; mentions the interplay with § 1988 fee shifting.
